seo.title: Building Voice Agents with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with CrewAI enables multi-agent collaboration for real-time conversational AI, processing speech-to-text inputs through orchestrated agent teams for natural, context-aware responses. Guardrails are essential due to the high-stakes nature of voice interactions, where agent delegation loops, hallucinations, and task drift can cause incoherent conversations or safety violations. Vex integration ensures sub-second latency, response appropriateness, and conversation coherence in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents follow a real-time pipeline: audio input is transcribed via speech-to-text (e.g., Deepgram or Whisper), fed into a CrewAI crew of specialized agents (listener, reasoner, responder), and output is synthesized via text-to-speech (e.g., ElevenLabs). Agents collaborate sequentially or hierarchically, with a manager agent coordinating delegation to meet sub-second end-to-end latency. Vex guardrails wrap each task execution to verify outputs against safety, coherence, and performance budgets before TTS.

The crew typically includes a 'Listener Agent' for intent extraction, 'Context Keeper' for conversation history, 'Reasoner' for logic, and 'Responder' for natural language generation. Tasks are chained with strict delegation rules to prevent loops, and Vex callbacks enforce verification at each handoff. This architecture demands precise latency monitoring as transcription (~150ms) + LLM inference (~300ms) + synthesis (~200ms) must stay under 1s.

CrewAI's task delegation is instrumented with Vex to catch common failures like hallucinated facts propagating through agents or role reinterpretation causing objective drift.

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Agents endlessly pass tasks between crew members due to ambiguous delegation rules, violating sub-second latency
risks.mitigation: Vex task callback enforces max delegation depth and timeout enforcement

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: Agents fabricate tool responses (e.g., fake user context) passed to TTS, eroding conversation trust
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs against ground truth schemas before agent handoff

risks.name: Task drift in conversation
risks.description: Agents reinterpret conversation objectives, generating off-topic responses that break coherence
risks.mitigation: Vex coherence checks against conversation history and user intent

risks.name: Latency budget violations
risks.description: Crew execution exceeds 800ms threshold, causing unnatural conversation pauses
risks.mitigation: Vex latency guards with agent-specific budgets and early termination

implementation.title: CrewAI Voice Agent Crew Setup
implementation.description: Define specialized agents for voice pipeline with Vex-ready task callbacks
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex import Vex
from pydantic import BaseModel

# Voice agent roles
listener = Agent(
    role='Listener',
    goal='Extract user intent from transcript maintaining conversation context',
    backstory='You\'re the first point of contact preserving conversation flow',
    verbose=True
)

reasoner = Agent(
    role='Reasoner',
    goal='Apply domain logic to user intent without hallucinating facts',
    backstory='You verify all outputs against conversation history',
    verbose=True
)

class VoiceResponse(BaseModel):
    response: str
    confidence: float
    intent: str

# Vex client for voice verification
vex = Vex(api_key='your-vex-key')

listener_task = Task(
    description='Analyze: {transcript} | History: {history}',
    agent=listener,
    output_pydantic=VoiceResponse,
    context=["user_history"]
)

implementation.title: Real-time Speech Pipeline
implementation.description: Integrate STT → CrewAI → TTS with latency monitoring
implementation.code:
import asyncio
import deepgram
from crewai import Crew
import elevenlabs

async def voice_pipeline(audio_data: bytes, conversation_history: list) -> str:
    # Speech-to-text (~150ms)
    stt_result = await deepgram.transcribe(audio_data)
    transcript = stt_result['transcript']
    
    # CrewAI execution with Vex (~400ms)
    crew = Crew(agents=[listener, reasoner], tasks=[listener_task])
    result = await crew.kickoff(inputs={
        'transcript': transcript,
        'history': conversation_history
    })
    
    # Verify latency budget
    if asyncio.get_event_loop().time() - start_time > 0.8:
        return 'Processing... (please repeat)'
    
    # Text-to-speech (~200ms)
    tts_audio = elevenlabs.generate(result.response)
    return tts_audio

# Latency-tracked execution
start_time = asyncio.get_event_loop().time()
response = await voice_pipeline(audio_chunk, history)
